story: I was promised a hotel job abroad by a recruiter who took my passport on arrival and made me work without pay. I kept a copy of the fake offer letter, and that document is what finally helped investigators act.
whatHelped: Keeping a copy of the recruitment paperwork and reaching the overseas workers cell through a helpline.

story: I was moved between cities and told no one would believe me. A survivor who had spoken up before was assigned as my peer supporter, and hearing that someone had gotten out gave me the courage to give a statement.
whatHelped: Peer support from another survivor and a trauma-informed officer who let me speak at my own pace.

story: I worked at a brick kiln to pay off a loan that never seemed to shrink. A labour inspector visited after a neighbour reported the site, and my whole family was released.
whatHelped: A witness reporting the site, and a labour inspectorate that treated the debt as illegal bondage.

story: I started working in a workshop at eleven. An NGO doing rounds found me, got me out, and helped me return to school. I now speak so other children are found sooner.
whatHelped: An NGO outreach team and a rehabilitation plan that included going back to school.

story: I was going to be married before I finished school. I called a child helpline quietly, and a counsellor spoke to my family with me so it did not become dangerous.
whatHelped: A confidential helpline and a mediator who protected me while talking to my family.

story: I thought I was in a relationship until I realised I was being sold. A hotline gave me a safety plan first, and only later helped me talk to investigators when I was ready.
whatHelped: A safety plan before any report, and being allowed to decide when to involve the police.
